context: The famous closing line encouraging graduates to follow their curiosity and intuition.

text: Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life.
context: Part of the 'connecting the dots' and mortality story urging authenticity.

text: Don't be trapped by dogma — which is living with the results of other people's thinking.
context: Warning against following others' paths instead of your own intuition.

text: And the only way to do great work is to love what you do. If you haven't found it yet, keep looking. Don't settle.
context: Core advice on career and passion from the 'love what you do' story.

text: You can't connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backwards.
context: The calligraphy class story showing how seemingly irrelevant experiences connect later.

text: Remembering that you are going to die is the best way I know to avoid the trap of thinking you have something to lose.
context: The death story encouraging courage to follow your heart.

text: Innovation distinguishes between a leader and a follower.
context: Core belief driving Apple's relentless focus on design and new ideas.

text: Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works.
context: Emphasizing that great rhetoric in tech is backed by substance and user experience.

text: Your work is going to fill a large part of your life, and the only way to be truly satisfied is to do what you believe is great work.
context: Emphasizing passion and excellence in one's chosen path.

text: Sometimes life hits you in the head with a brick. Don't lose faith.
context: Resilience in the face of setbacks, drawn from his firing from Apple story.
